breaking_news.title: Iran-linked Handala Hackers Breach FBI Director's Personal Email
breaking_news.summary:
Iran-linked hacking group Handala successfully breached the personal Gmail account of FBI Director Kash Patel, leaking a cache of photos and documents to the internet. The FBI confirmed the incident, stating that the exposed material is historical in nature and does not involve any government or classified information.

This breach highlights the targeting of high-profile government officials by state-sponsored actors, even when personal accounts are involved. While no sensitive government data was compromised, the incident underscores the persistent threat landscape facing top law enforcement leadership.

breaking_news.title: ShinyHunters Claims 350GB Data Breach at European Commission
breaking_news.summary:
The European Commission confirmed a cyberattack on its cloud infrastructure hosting the Europa.eu platform, with the ShinyHunters extortion group claiming responsibility for the breach. The group alleges they stole over 350 GB of data, including mail servers, databases, and confidential internal communications.

The attack targeted Amazon Web Services accounts associated with the Commission, though AWS stated their services operated as designed. This incident represents a significant compromise of EU executive body infrastructure, prompting an ongoing investigation into the full scope of the data theft.

breaking_news.title: F5 BIG-IP Critical RCE Flaw Added to CISA KEV Catalog
breaking_news.summary:
A critical vulnerability in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager (CVE-2025-53521) has been reclassified from a denial-of-service issue to a Remote Code Execution flaw with a CVSS score of 9.8. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation.

Attackers can trigger remote code execution when an access policy is enabled on a virtual server, posing a severe risk to network infrastructure. Organizations using F5 BIG-IP APM are urged to apply patches immediately to prevent unauthorized access and potential system compromise.

breaking_news.title: Russia-linked APT TA446 Uses DarkSword Exploit to Target iPhone Users
breaking_news.summary:
Russia-linked APT group TA446 is actively using the DarkSword iOS exploit kit in targeted spear-phishing campaigns to compromise iPhone users. The attacks rely on malicious emails to deliver the exploit, marking a significant escalation in threats against mobile devices from state-sponsored actors.

TA446 primarily targets defense, intelligence, and NGO sectors in NATO countries, the Baltics, and Eastern Europe. This campaign demonstrates the evolving capabilities of APT groups to leverage leaked exploit kits for sophisticated mobile espionage operations.

breaking_news.title: China-linked Red Menshen APT Deploys Stealthy Implants in Telecom Networks
breaking_news.summary:
China-linked APT group Red Menshen has been deploying stealthy BPFDoor implants within telecom networks to maintain hidden access for long-term espionage. Rapid7 Labs uncovered this campaign, which has been active since at least 2021 and primarily targets government communications in the Middle East and Asia.

These implants act as digital sleeper cells embedded deep within critical infrastructure, allowing attackers to monitor communications without detection. The compromise of telecom networks threatens entire populations by exposing call records and sensitive digital identities to state-sponsored surveillance.

breaking_news.title: Citrix NetScaler Critical Flaw Under Active Reconnaissance by Attackers
breaking_news.summary:
Attackers are actively probing a critical vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway, tracked as CVE-2026-3055, which allows unauthenticated remote attackers to leak sensitive data. The flaw is a memory overread issue with a CVSS score of 9.3, triggered specifically when the appliance is configured as a SAML IDP.

Citrix has issued security updates for this vulnerability, but organizations must verify their configurations to ensure they are not exposed. Active reconnaissance indicates that threat actors are scanning for vulnerable instances to exploit the data leakage potential before patches are widely deployed.

breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Compromises Telnyx PyPI Package in Supply Chain Attack
breaking_news.summary:
The threat actor TeamPCP has compromised the Telnyx package on the Python Package Index (PyPI), uploading malicious versions that deliver credential-stealing malware. The malware is concealed within a WAV audio file, making it difficult to detect during standard package installation processes.

This supply chain attack follows previous compromises of Trivy and KICS, indicating a persistent campaign against developer ecosystems. Users of the affected Telnyx versions are at risk of having sensitive credentials harvested, necessitating immediate removal and rotation of exposed secrets.

breaking_news.title: Apple Issues Urgent Lock Screen Warnings for Unpatched iPhones
breaking_news.summary:
Apple is sending urgent lock screen notifications to users of outdated iPhones and iPads, warning them of active web-based attacks targeting their devices. The alerts urge users to install critical software updates immediately to protect against exploitation of known vulnerabilities in older iOS versions.

This proactive measure highlights the severity of ongoing exploitation campaigns against unpatched mobile devices. Users running older software versions are at significant risk, as attackers are actively leveraging these weaknesses to compromise devices through web-based vectors.
